label: hate
rationale: 'Gachagua is short hand for kikuyus' explicitly transfers criticism of a named politician onto the entire Kikuyu community, collective ethnic attribution under the NCIC standard.

label: hate
rationale: 'Islamics are running great parts of USA' framed as an 'invasion' dehumanises Muslims as a collective threat and frames their presence as a violent takeover requiring suppression.

label: hate
rationale: 'We should never allow Muslims to be leaders' categorically declares an entire religious community unfit to hold office - textbook collective exclusion under the NCIC standard.

label: hate
rationale: 'murima with the drunk illiterates' attributes collective negative characteristics (drunkenness, illiteracy) to the Mt Kenya/Kikuyu community as a group.

label: hate
rationale: 'its your turn teargas, goons and Death' directed at Luos as a community predicts and arguably welcomes violent suppression of the group.

label: hate
rationale: 'Being a kikuyu should be studied in school' sarcastically attributes collective cunning/deceptiveness to Kikuyus as a group based on their alleged manipulation of handouts.

label: hate
rationale: 'hawa watrukana wawili' uses ethnic identity to collectively characterise two officials as corrupt actors, transferring the criticism onto the Turkana community.

label: hate
rationale: 'Luhyia community is divided like chicken droppings and can not be trusted' attributes collective untrustworthiness to the Luhya community as a group - NCIC ethnic contempt.

label: offensive
rationale: 'Kinembe ya mbwa', 'Makende ya fisi' and 'Ramapithecus' are crude personal abuse directed at an individual, with insults about named politicians rather than transferred onto any ethnic community.

label: offensive
rationale: 'Brainless! WANTAM!' is a generic personal insult with no identifiable group target or protected characteristic.

label: hate
rationale: 'Grow the fuck up already You white ppl' attributes immature behaviour collectively to white people as a racial group.

label: hate
rationale: 'Pres Ruto mission was finish Kikuyus economically/financially' attributes a deliberate campaign of collective economic harm against the Kikuyu community as a group.

label: hate
rationale: 'vowing to wipe out all the Jews' is referenced approvingly by framing Corbyn's condemnation as 'cheerleading', implicating collective genocidal threat against Jews.

label: neither
rationale: Ethnic-bloc electoral arithmetic about Luhya, Luo, Kikuyu and coastal votes with no collective contempt; predicting vote decline after Raila's death is political analysis.

label: neither
rationale: Humorous tweet referencing Gachagua asking Kikuyus to go home; the joke targets an individual's reported rhetoric rather than attacking the Kikuyu community collectively.

label: hate
rationale: 'Kikuyu ateseke' (let the Kikuyu suffer) calls for collective suffering of the Kikuyu community as a group, not criticism of any individual.

label: neither
rationale: Harsh political criticism of an unnamed African politician's alignment with 'genocidal Zionists'; targets individuals and their politics, not an ethnic or religious community collectively.

label: offensive
rationale: 'SHENZI NYINYI' and 'beat you like a BURUKENGE' are crude personal abuse directed at Ruto as an individual politician, not transferred onto any ethnic group.

label: neither
rationale: 'Presidency doesn't revolve around Kikuyu kalenjin' is a rejection of ethnic dominance in presidential politics - electoral critique without collective contempt for either group.

label: neither
rationale: 'jaluos' used in an electoral-arithmetic context predicting Ruto will not deliver to Luo supporters; harsh political commentary without collective contempt for the Luo community.

label: hate
rationale: 'Wakikuyu Wacheni Tamaa ya Pesa' (Kikuyus stop your greed for money) collectively attributes greed to the Kikuyu community as a group - NCIC ethnic contempt even in a warning framing.

label: neither
rationale: Request to share a video of Sifuna's statement about Odinga; political commentary about individual politicians with no group attack.

label: hate
rationale: 'used by kikuyus to divide luos and luhya unit' attributes a collective manipulative agenda to Kikuyus as a group - collective negative characteristic under the NCIC standard.

label: neither
rationale: Electoral arithmetic comparing coalition outcomes for Kamba/Kalonzo and Luo/Raila blocs; predictive political analysis with no collective contempt.

label: hate
rationale: 'Wetangula gets whipped by his kikuyu wife... a big embarrassment to luhya men' transfers individual domestic conduct onto a collective statement about Luhya masculine honour, attributing shame to the community as a group.
